statement.en: The Decoupling Mechanism as a Boundary Condition: Geometric Resolution to the Dark Sector and Hubble Tensions
statement.summary: We test whether radiative decoupling surfaces admit a scale-independent geometric normalization that can be evaluated at stellar photospheres and projected to the cosmic horizon. By coupling independently measured radiative observables with Newtonian surface gravity, we define a dimensionless boundary invariant, X. Using a benchmark sample of 190 detached eclipsing-binary (DEB) components, we find that stellar photospheres cluster near the projected blackbody phase-space capacity X₀ = π³/15 ≈ 2.067. While evaluating this invariant using standard macroscopic relations yields an algebraic tautology, this strict circularity mathematically demonstrates that standard physical laws inherently force mass and gravity to perfectly cancel at the radiative boundary, leaving a pure geometric invariant

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper presents a provisional theoretical claim (radiative decoupling boundary invariant X₀ = π³/15) supported by empirical clustering in 190 DEB components, but the core argument contains a acknowledged tautological structure when evaluated macroscopically. This is preprint-stage work (2026-04-25, arXiv category) with mathematical circularity requiring independent validation and resolution of whether the tautology is a proof of deep constraint or an artifact of the formalism.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 境界で質量と重力が消え、幾何学だけが残る。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 内なる質量が消滅し、外殻だけが宇宙と対話する。
